reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

claims.statement: `$request->validate()` evaluates request input against the supplied validation rules.
claims.sourceLocator: Validation Quickstart; Writing the Validation Logic

claims.statement: When validation passes, the method returns the validated data.
When validation fails, Laravel redirects traditional web requests with errors and returns a JSON error response for XHR requests.
claims.sourceLocator: Writing the Validation Logic; XHR Requests and Validation

verifications.method: Submit a payload that satisfies every rule and inspect the returned validated array.
verifications.expectedResult: Only rule-covered validated values are returned.

verifications.method: Submit a traditional web request missing a required field and inspect the redirect and error session.
verifications.expectedResult: Laravel redirects back with validation errors and old input as configured.

verifications.method: Submit an equivalent JSON/XHR request and inspect the error status and JSON body.
verifications.expectedResult: Laravel returns the documented validation error response for the JSON request.

limitations: Validation rules do not establish authorization or make downstream output safe for every context.
Database races and cross-record invariants may require additional transactional checks.
Independent human review remains required before any status promotion.
